pain_strength.evidence: Seasonal bulk orders create FX cost uncertainty.

urgency.evidence: Next buying season approaching; FX budgets being set.

competitor_context.evidence: Bank FX with no specialist provider; paying wide spreads.

Merchant Acquiring.explanation: 12 retail stores plus flagship e-commerce; payment acceptance is core to the business.

product_fit.evidence: 12 stores plus e-commerce; payments are core.

pain_strength.evidence: Processing costs are high relative to fashion margins.

urgency.evidence: Reviewing costs but no active PSP switch project.

competitor_context.evidence: Barclaycard for in-store; Stripe for online.

Revolut Pay.explanation: Online sales are 45% of revenue; actively testing new checkout flows to reduce basket abandonment.

product_fit.evidence: 45% online revenue; checkout optimisation is active.

commercial_value.evidence: Higher conversion on £9M online revenue is material.

pain_strength.evidence: Basket abandonment is a tracked KPI.

urgency.evidence: Digital team actively A/B testing checkout.

competitor_context.evidence: Offers PayPal and Klarna; room for Revolut Pay.

Spend Management.explanation: Seasonal buying cycles mean large procurement spikes; some budget control need but existing processes work.

product_fit.evidence: Seasonal procurement spikes.

pain_strength.evidence: Processes work during normal periods; strain during peaks.

urgency.evidence: Not a current priority.

competitor_context.evidence: Manual process; no incumbent tool.

competitors.name: Bank FX
competitors.notes: No specialist provider. Paying wide spreads on seasonal orders.

competitors.name: Barclaycard / Stripe
competitors.notes: Split between in-store (Barclaycard) and online (Stripe).

competitors.name: Klarna
competitors.notes: BNPL offered at checkout alongside PayPal.

stakeholders.name: Isabella Thompson
stakeholders.notes: Reviews all payment provider costs. Seasonal FX budgets.

stakeholders.name: Lucas Martin
stakeholders.notes: A/B testing checkout flows. Open to new payment options.

cadence_history.summary: Revolut Pay pitch to Digital Director.
Demo of Revolut Pay checkout integration.

signals: Digital Director replied to Revolut Pay pitch

merchant_spend.wallet_opportunity: High — £12M split between 12 stores and e-commerce. Growing online channel

FX.explanation: Multi-national operations in 8 countries; EUR, USD, SEK, and NOK payment flows. Treasury team manages £40M+ in annual FX.

product_fit.evidence: 8-country operations; £40M+ annual FX flows.

commercial_value.evidence: £55M turnover; largest FX opportunity in pipeline.

pain_strength.evidence: Complex multi-currency treasury with high transaction costs.

urgency.evidence: Treasury modernisation programme underway.

competitor_context.evidence: Barclays and SEB; multiple relationships but poor consolidation.

FX Forwards.explanation: Long-term infrastructure contracts with known future FX obligations; formal hedging policy in place.

product_fit.evidence: Infrastructure contracts with multi-year FX obligations.

pain_strength.evidence: Current hedging through banks is expensive and slow to execute.

urgency.evidence: Formal hedging policy review mandated by board.

competitor_context.evidence: Bank forward contracts; functional but uncompetitive pricing.

API Integrations.explanation: SAP-based treasury; automation interest exists but IT priorities are focused on operational systems.

product_fit.evidence: SAP treasury with automation potential.

pain_strength.evidence: Manual processes but IT focused elsewhere.

urgency.evidence: Not on near-term IT roadmap.

competitor_context.evidence: SAP native integrations exist.

Cards.explanation: 600 staff across multiple sites and countries; significant travel, fuel, and field procurement needs.

product_fit.evidence: 600 staff across multiple countries with field spend.

commercial_value.evidence: Large cardholder base with high per-head spend.

pain_strength.evidence: Mixed card and expense processes across countries.

urgency.evidence: Consolidation project on radar but not started.

competitor_context.evidence: Different cards per country; fragmented.

Spend Management.explanation: Multi-entity, multi-country structure with per-project budgets; finance team struggles with consolidation.

product_fit.evidence: Multi-entity, multi-country project-based budgeting.

pain_strength.evidence: Finance team manually consolidates across entities monthly.

urgency.evidence: CFO pushing for better financial controls.

competitor_context.evidence: Excel and email; no centralised spend tool.

Monthly Plans.explanation: Enterprise-scale usage justifies custom pricing; currently on standard plan with workarounds for multi-entity access.

product_fit.evidence: Enterprise scale with multi-entity needs.

commercial_value.evidence: Custom plan for 600+ users is very high value.

pain_strength.evidence: Working around plan limitations across entities.

urgency.evidence: Would follow primary product adoption.

competitor_context.evidence: No competing plan structure.

competitors.name: Barclays
competitors.notes: Primary banking relationship. Poor FX consolidation across entities.

competitors.name: SEB
competitors.notes: Nordic operations. Adequate but no global view.

competitors.name: Bank forwards
competitors.notes: Functional but uncompetitive pricing and slow execution.

stakeholders.name: Anna Bergstrom
stakeholders.notes: Leading treasury modernisation programme. Key decision maker.

stakeholders.name: David Fletcher
stakeholders.notes: Pushing for better financial controls and cost visibility.

stakeholders.name: Raj Patel
stakeholders.notes: SAP treasury integration interest. IT priorities focused on ops.

cadence_history.summary: Intro via conference contact to Group Treasurer.
Initial treasury review meeting.
FX and forwards deep-dive with treasury team.
Comprehensive FX and forwards proposal sent.
Proposal review with CFO and Treasurer.
Revised pricing and implementation timeline sent.

signals: CFO and Treasurer reviewed proposal positively

FX.explanation: Imports components from Germany and Japan; moderate FX volumes but growing as EU sourcing increases.

product_fit.evidence: Germany and Japan imports with EUR and JPY payments.

pain_strength.evidence: FX costs are noticeable but manageable.

urgency.evidence: EU sourcing increasing; FX need growing.

competitor_context.evidence: Bank transfers for imports; standard pricing.

Merchant Acquiring.explanation: Operates a trade counter and small online catalogue; card payments are a minority of revenue but growing.

product_fit.evidence: Trade counter and small online catalogue.

commercial_value.evidence: Card payments are minority of revenue.

pain_strength.evidence: Current terminal works; costs are acceptable.

urgency.evidence: No plans to change.

competitor_context.evidence: iZettle terminal; adequate for needs.

Cards.explanation: Field sales team and warehouse operatives with purchasing needs; ad-hoc spending is poorly controlled.

product_fit.evidence: Field sales and warehouse teams with purchasing needs.

pain_strength.evidence: Ad-hoc spending poorly tracked.

urgency.evidence: Finance team wants better controls.

competitor_context.evidence: No corporate card programme.

competitors.name: Bank transfers
competitors.notes: Standard bank for imports. No specialist FX.

competitors.name: iZettle
competitors.notes: Simple terminal for trade counter. Adequate for current scale.

stakeholders.name: Gary Wilson
stakeholders.notes: Hands-on MD. Finance team wants better controls on ad-hoc spend.

signals: Finance team wants better controls but no champion identified

merchant_spend.wallet_opportunity: Low — £420K mostly trade counter transactions. Limited card payment adoption

Cards.explanation: Multi-academy trust with 6 schools; each school has departmental purchasing needs currently handled via purchase orders and petty cash.

product_fit.evidence: 6 schools with departmental purchasing needs.

pain_strength.evidence: Purchase orders and petty cash are slow and error-prone.

urgency.evidence: Trustees pushing for operational modernisation.

competitor_context.evidence: No corporate card programme; PO-based process.

Spend Management.explanation: Complex budget delegation across schools and departments; trustees want real-time visibility into spending against allocated budgets.

product_fit.evidence: Multi-school budget delegation with oversight needs.

pain_strength.evidence: Trustees demanding real-time spend visibility.

urgency.evidence: Board resolution to implement controls by year-end.

competitor_context.evidence: No spend management tool; manual reconciliation.

Monthly Plans.explanation: Transaction volumes and user count justify a plan upgrade, but procurement process for financial services is slow.

product_fit.evidence: Scale justifies plan upgrade.

pain_strength.evidence: Current plan mostly adequate.

urgency.evidence: Procurement process is slow for financial services.

competitor_context.evidence: Would follow from primary product adoption.

competitors.name: Purchase orders
competitors.notes: PO-based process with petty cash. Slow and error-prone.

competitors.name: Manual reconciliation
competitors.notes: No software. Manual budget tracking across 6 schools.

stakeholders.name: Dr. Patricia Moore
stakeholders.notes: Driving operational modernisation across the trust.

stakeholders.name: Steven Clarke
stakeholders.notes: Board resolution to implement spend controls by year-end.

stakeholders.name: Amanda Price
stakeholders.notes: Manages procurement for the largest academy in the trust.
